bullets.summary: NVDA reported Q3 FY2026 revenue of $57.0B, with data center revenue of $51.2B (record), and guided Q4 to $65.0B (±2%). Blackwell GPU demand described by CEO Jensen Huang as 'off the charts'; cloud GPU allocations reported sold out. Stock up ~40% for 2025, trading $187-$190 in late December.

Mid-December sentiment showed a tension between undeniable Blackwell infrastructure buildout evidence and rising skepticism about whether future dominance is already priced in. Analyst forecasts in December noted a $0.5T Blackwell/Rubin pipeline and projected 40% long-term revenue CAGR.

Google launched Gemini 3 Flash in December 2025, making it the default model in the Gemini app. The model incorporates Gemini 3 Pro reasoning capabilities at a lower cost and faster speed, positioning Google as having closed its perceived AI gap versus OpenAI.

Year-end narrative: Google 'started 2025 behind in the AI race and ended 2025 on top,' driven by Gemini model cadence, AI Pro/Ultra pricing tiers, and NotebookLM growth. Secondary quantum exposure: Google's Willow chip (announced Dec 2024) continued to anchor GOOGL's quantum credibility heading into 2025 year-end.

Microsoft launched Microsoft 365 Copilot Business (targeting SMBs under 300 users at $21/user/month) in December 2025, with a 15% promotional discount running through March 2026. Azure Copilot announced as an agentic cloud-management interface at Ignite 2025.

Q2 FY2026 (quarter ending Dec 31, 2025): Microsoft spent approximately $37.5B on AI infrastructure, placing it on a ~$150B annualized capex run rate. AI infrastructure spending signals continued commitment but also raises questions about near-term margin pressure.

Rocket Lab awarded an $816M prime contract by the U.S. Space Development Agency on Dec 19, 2025 to design and build 18 Tracking Layer Tranche 3 (TRKT3) missile-defense satellites. Combined with a prior $515M SDA award, RKLB now holds over $1.3B in SDA contract value. Stock surged ~22% on the announcement.
RKLB ended 2025 with 21 Electron launches and 100% mission success rate, establishing a track record that supports the company's transition from launch provider to satellite prime contractor.

The Robot Report's Top 10 Robotics Developments of December 2025 highlighted humanoid entry into commercial use: Mercado Libre signed an agreement to deploy Agility Robotics' Digit humanoid in its San Antonio facility; iREX 2025 trade show set attendance records with 673 exhibitors and 156,110 visitors, with order intake recovering after two years of decline.

A Motley Fool analysis published Dec 30, 2025 examined whether BOTZ could triple in three years, citing the global robotics market valued at ~$108B in 2025 with forecasts of ~$416B by 2035. The ETF closed 2025 at $36.55, with BOTZ holdings spanning industrial automation and AI-enabled robot makers.

ARKG returned approximately -7.1% in December 2025, underperforming the health category average of -1.8% for the month. Despite the December pullback, the fund posted ~23% for full-year 2025, outpacing broader health benchmarks.

Top ARKG holdings as of December 2025: CRISPR Therapeutics (9.71%), Twist Bioscience (9.68%), Tempus AI (9.58%), 10x Genomics (6.64%), Beam Therapeutics (5.52%). ARK Invest disclosed active trading in ARKG holdings in early December, consistent with tactical rebalancing.

BTQ Technologies joined the Defiance Quantum ETF (QTUM) effective December 15, 2025, following the semi-annual reconstitution of the BlueStar Machine Learning and Quantum Computing Index (BQTUM). BTQ entered via a strategic Keypair IP acquisition.

Quantum Computing Report's year-end piece noted 2025 as 'the end of the hype-fueled easy era,' with serious hardware milestones (IBM, Google, Microsoft error-correction progress) alongside a call to distinguish genuine breakthroughs from incremental claims. The wave is advancing but commercial applications remain pre-revenue for most players.

NUKZ (Range Nuclear Renaissance Index ETF) returned approximately 56.58% for full-year 2025, driven by AI data-center power demand, Trump administration support for nuclear, and bipartisan legislative momentum. Energy Secretary Chris Wright gave a high-profile speech at Idaho National Laboratory in early December underscoring White House backing.

GE Vernova Hitachi Nuclear Energy announced a strategic alliance with Samsung C&T in December 2025 to advance deployment of the BWRX-300 small modular reactor in Sweden (five units planned). This is a concrete SMR commercial partnership, a key constituent theme for NUKZ.

The FOMC cut the federal funds rate 25 bps on December 10, 2025 to a target range of 3.50-3.75%, the third cut of the year. The dot plot signaled only one additional cut in 2026 and one in 2027, implying the easing cycle is near its end. The 10-year Treasury yield traded near 4.3% at year-end.

BIL (SPDR Bloomberg 1-3 Month T-Bill ETF) traded at approximately $91.50 as of early December 2025, returning ~4.23% over the trailing year. The December FOMC 25 bps cut to 3.50-3.75% will modestly reduce forward yield. The dot plot's signal of only one 2026 cut limits further yield compression, keeping BIL's yield attractive relative to pre-2022 norms.

Gold (tracked by IAU) was one of 2025's standout performers, with GLD gaining approximately 27% for the year versus ~8% for SPY. Gold reached new record highs in Q2 2025 (seventh consecutive quarterly high) and continued to be bid as a geopolitical hedge and inflation store. IAU's 52-week range through year-end was roughly $59.71-$104.40.

VIG (Vanguard Dividend Appreciation ETF) tracks the S&P US Dividend Growers Index, selecting stocks with at least 10 consecutive years of dividend growth. The fund returned approximately 19% in 2024 but underperformed its category index by 7 percentage points that year. No material December-specific corporate actions surfaced for VIG in the lookback window; it functions as a quality-equity anchor in the portfolio.

AI.rationale: All three AI names (NVDA, GOOGL, MSFT) show real, compounding revenue — NVDA Q3 data-center at $51.2B, MSFT on a $150B annualized AI capex run rate — but December headlines explicitly flag that NVDA's upside may already be priced in, analyst notes are unanimous on demand, and MSFT's capex scale raises near-term margin questions. Sell-side sentiment is uniformly bullish; new entrants are chasing; valuations are stretched. These are peak-cycle symptoms.

rockets_spacecraft.rationale: RKLB's $816M SDA contract win and 100% Electron launch success in 2025 show the transition from launch startup to credible defense prime is now generating real, large-scale revenue and backlog. The wave is clearly past buildup but has not yet hit peak enthusiasm — the satellite prime role is new and the Neutron rocket program still represents significant upside. Adoption is compounding with room to run.

robotics.rationale: BOTZ's December 2025 backdrop shows order intake just beginning to recover after two years of decline, and humanoid robots are entering commercial use for the first time (Mercado Libre/Digit deployment). The iREX trade show set records, signaling rising industry attention, but actual commercial revenue from humanoids remains early-stage. This is the quiet, cheap, under-owned phase of the humanoid sub-wave.

engineered_biology.rationale: ARKG fell 7.1% in December 2025 against a category average of -1.8%, reflecting sector-level selling pressure despite strong full-year gains. The genomics/gene-editing wave surged in earlier years (especially 2020-2021) and is now in a multi-year digestion. Underlying science is advancing but commercial timelines are long and investor patience is short.

quantum.rationale: The Quantum Computing Report's year-end piece explicitly called out 'the end of the hype-fueled easy era,' suggesting the market is becoming more discriminating. QTUM reconstituted to add BTQ Technologies but players remain pre-revenue. Hardware milestones (IBM, Google, Microsoft) are real but commercial applications are still years away. Classic buildup: genuine scientific progress, under-owned by the broad market.

nuclear_fusion.rationale: NUKZ returned 56.58% for 2025 — that is a surge-level return. The catalysts are concrete: government backing, AI data-center power demand pulling in nuclear utilities, and SMR commercial partnerships (GE Vernova Hitachi / Samsung C&T BWRX-300 in Sweden). This is not purely speculative enthusiasm; revenue and policy tailwinds are real. However the 56% annual return warrants monitoring for approaching peak.

general_markets.rationale: AGG, BIL, IAU, and VIG are macro/asset-class instruments without a technology-wave thesis. The December FOMC cut and gold's strong 2025 run are noteworthy context but do not map to a wave stage.

thematic_concerns.concern: The investor profile calls for trimming AI exposure before the crest. December 2025 headlines flag unanimous sell-side bullishness, funding-durability skepticism, and valuation stretch — classic pre-crest signals. The wave_stage call of 'peak' directly triggers the profile's 'trim before it crests' operating rule.
thematic_concerns.evidence: ts2.tech Dec 18 2025: 'opposing forces — undeniable evidence of ongoing AI infrastructure buildouts versus rising skepticism about funding durability and how much future dominance is already priced in.'

thematic_concerns.concern: Same AI-peak concern as NVDA. $37.5B quarterly AI capex (~$150B annualized) is a margin risk if AI monetization slows. Profile rule 1 applies: ride the AI wave but trim before it crests.
thematic_concerns.evidence: tech-insider.org: Microsoft on a $150B annualized AI capex run rate as of Q2 FY2026 (ending Dec 31, 2025).

thematic_concerns.concern: NUKZ is mapped to the nuclear_fusion wave bucket by ticker theme, but the ETF's actual holdings are primarily fission utilities, fuel, and construction — not pure-play fusion. The profile's 'nuclear fusion' next-wave thesis is pre-commercial; NUKZ captures near-term fission/SMR exposure rather than fusion directly. This mismatch should be noted when interpreting the surge stage for the fusion wave.
thematic_concerns.evidence: Range Nuclear Renaissance ETF fund description: 'advanced reactors, utilities, construction & services, and fuel' — fission-dominant holdings, not fusion startups.
